hint: Use two transistors as independent controlled switches sharing the same enable line. T1: control=C, data=A → Out1 = A·C. T2: control=C, data=B → Out2 = B·C. Enable signal C gates both paths.
title: Gated Signal Pair
objective: Gate two signals with a shared enable
description: Use two transistors to gate a pair of data signals with one enable line. When Enable (C) is HIGH, signals A and B pass through to their respective outputs. When Enable is LOW, both outputs are forced to 0. This is how enable lines work in real bus drivers.
